title: Cryptographic Randomness, Entropy, Keys, Salts, and Nonces · Execution Trace: Crypto Go Nonce Uniqueness
summary: A service seeds math/rand with time, generates keys and reset tokens from it, retries by reusing a nonce, and cannot prove fork or startup entropy behavior. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the nonce uniqueness increment in randomness and uniqueness contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence de…
objectives: Enforce construction-specific nonce uniqueness or misuse limits across concurrency, restart, rollback, and replication.
Generate algorithm-compatible keys at supported strengths and retain provenance, purpose, owner, and creation evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Nonce Uniqueness · Theory Crypto Go Nonce Uniqueness
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 6752 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to budget cryptographic randomness and nonce uniqueness across use, restart, and rollback; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
